title: Dr Liz O’Riordan: The breast cancer surgeon dispelling the myths
summary: “I was a 40-year-old breast cancer surgeon who never thought I’d get breast cancer and never checked my breasts,” is how Dr Liz O’Riordan begins when I ask what brought her to write her new book, The Cancer Roadmap. We are talking in advance of Daffodil Day, the national day of fundraising for the Irish Cancer Society taking place on Friday.

title: This rising baker was 23 when multiple sclerosis limited her mobility. Now she helps others find their way
summary: After Ms Cheryl Tan displayed the first signs of a debilitating neurological condition at age 23, daily tasks such as taking a bus soon became daunting. Now, in her work with bus operator Tower Transit Singapore, she is trying to make public transport more inclusive for all.
